title: Align Python link signing with protocol framing
description: Independent review found Python link_layer.py signing/framing does not match the link draft or the Rust/C implementations: signable data omits length/LLSec fields and signatures are appended as payload with placeholder MIC framing. Confirm the normative wire format and implement cross-implementation-compatible signing with independent vectors/tests.
notes: Compatibility decision: the normative contract is LENGTH || LLSec || EPO || SEQ || DST || PLD; S=1 places the full 48-byte Schnorr signature in MIC. MicLength remains 4/8-byte MIC selection only for S=0. Updated Python, Rust, C, draft, and vectors/tests. Verified: uv run pytest tests/link -q (145 passed, 4 skipped); cargo test -p lichen-link (32 tests passed including shared vectors); git diff --check passed. Zephyr C tests were not run locally: no configured CMake build directory; Linux/EC2 west build and link_crypto/schnorr48 Twister verification remain required.
close_reason: Implemented and locally verified cross-implementation framing/signing alignment; Zephyr verification documented as Linux/EC2-required.
